article_title: With $3.5B in fresh capital, Kleiner Perkins is going all in on AI
article_summary: The fundraise includes $1 billion for investing in early-stage startups, and $2.5 billion for late-stage growth businesses.

article_title: Databricks bought two startups to underpin its new AI security product
article_summary: With an overflowing war chest from its recent $5 billion raise, Databricks is buying startups and looking for more. It acquired Antimatter and SiftD.ai.

article_title: Doss raises $55M for AI inventory management that plugs into ERP
article_summary: Doss's AI-powered inventory management system integrates with existing ERP systems. The Series B round was co-led by Madrona and Premji Invest.

article_title: Mirage raises $75M to continue building models for its AI video-editing app Captions
article_summary: Mirage, the maker of video-editing app Captions, has raised $75 million in growth financing from General Catalyst's Customer Value Fund (CVF).

article_title: Air Street becomes one of the largest solo VCs in Europe with $232M fund
article_summary: London’s Air Street Capital has raised a large Fund III with eyes locked on backing early-stage European and North American AI companies.

article_title: Startup Gimlet Labs is solving the AI inference bottleneck in a surprisingly elegant way
article_summary: Gimlet Labs just raised an $80 million Series A for tech that lets AI run across NVIDIA, AMD, Intel, ARM, Cerebras and d-Matrix chips, simultaneously.

article_title: Littlebird raises $11M for its AI-assisted ‘recall’ tool that reads your computer screen
article_summary: Littlebird is building an AI that reads your screen in real time to capture context, answer questions, and automate tasks, without relying on screenshots.

article_title: Meridian raises $17 million to remake the agentic spreadsheet
article_summary: A new company called Meridian.AI has emerged from stealth with an IDE-based approach to agentic financial modeling.

article_title: Serval raises $47M to bring AI agents to IT service management
article_summary: Serval is using agentic AI models to automate IT service management, but the company has a unique approach that takes advantage of agentic AI’s powers while avoiding many of its pitfalls.

article_title: How 2 UC Berkeley dropouts raised $28M for their AI marketing automation startup
article_summary: A marketing automation startup called Conversation has a founding story that sounds like it could have been an episode of the HBO show “Silicon Valley.”

article_title: North America takes the bulk of AI VC investments, despite tough political environment
article_summary: Despite what some experts have characterized as an environment increasingly hostile to AI R&D, North America continues to receive the bulk of AI venture dollars.

article_title: Capsule captures $12M to build the next version of its AI video editor for brands
article_summary: Capsule is upgrading its AI-powered video editing assistant for marketing, sales, and media teams following the close of a $12 million round of Series A funding, the company announced on Wednesday. The upgraded editor will include new features like AI suggestions and support for real-time collaborat

article_title: Intangible, a no-code 3D creation tool for filmmakers and game designers, raises $4M
article_summary: Intangible, now backed by $4 million in seed funding, offers an AI-powered creative tool that allows users to create 3D world concepts with text prompts to aid creative professionals across a variety of industries. The company’s mission is to make the creative process accessible to everyone, i

article_title: Elon Musk’s AI company, xAI, said to be in talks to raise $10B
article_summary: Elon Musk’s AI company, xAI, is said to be in talks to raise $10 billion in a round that would value xAI at $75 billion. article_title: Kleiner Perkins Raises $3.5B For AI-Focused Funds
article_summary: Kleiner Perkins' fundraise includes $1 billion for KP22, a fund to back early-stage companies, and $2.5 billion targeted for growth-stage investments.

article_title: US Startup Funding Slows Sharply In March
article_summary: The slowdown is almost entirely due to fewer giant AI megarounds closing this month.

article_title: The Week’s 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: Investment Slows, But Security And AI Remain Top Picks
article_summary: The size of the largest U.S. deals this week was smaller than in recent weeks, and heavily featured cybersecurity- and privacy-focused startups. Other areas that attracted good-sized financings included AI infrastructure, biotech, healthcare, and robotics.

article_title: Don’t Just Talk About AI. Measure Business Outputs. Here’s How.
article_summary: Guest authors Bob Morse of Strattam Capital and Dario Fanucchi of Isazi, share their theory on why so many AI pilots fail, believing that the best solution to see measurable business improvements involves varying the tools until one moves the dial in a sustainable way.
